title: Urostachys saururus
taxonomy: Urostachys saururus is classified within the Lycopodiopsida order and the Selaginellaceae family. It belongs to the genus Urostachys, which is a group of small to medium-sized, terrestrial plants. Urostachys saururus is a species of spikemoss, characterized by its small size and unique branching patterns. As a member of the Selaginellaceae family, Urostachys saururus shares characteristics with other spikemosses, such as their simple, needle-like leaves. Further classification of Urostachys saururus involves its specific taxonomic rank within the plant kingdom.

intro: Urostachys saururus is a species of flowering plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae, commonly known as the 'Saururus fern'. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress.
